content:
name: No co-authored-by in commits
description: Never add Co-Authored-By lines to git commit messages
type: feedback
---

Never add "Co-Authored-By" lines to git commit messages.

**Why:** Julian finds it unnecessary/annoying — strong preference.

**How to apply:** When creating any git commit, omit the Co-Authored-By trailer entirely. No exceptions.

name: Decision-making priorities
description: Julian prioritizes reliability, quality, and maintainability over simplicity, effort, or time savings when making technical decisions
type: feedback
---

When recommending between technical approaches, prioritize reliability, quality, and maintainability — never let effort or time tip the scale if a more robust option would make a true difference to the project outcome.

**Why:** Julian explicitly corrected a recommendation that favored simplicity/lo

name: Jeans Marketplace Automation
description: Greenfield Node.js web tool for Marcel Valentin's friend to bulk-publish secondhand jeans to Shopify + eBay from Excel + ZIP upload. Ricardo.ch deferred. Hosted on Railway (MVP).
type: project
---

Web tool for a single seller to upload Excel (.xlsx) + ZIP of product photos and auto-publish jeans listings to Shopify and eBay.de.

**Why:** The seller currently lists jeans manually across platforms — hours of repetitive work per batch. This tool elim

name: Monolith OpenClaw Trading System
description: Marcel's personal multi-agent trading system on monolith server (100.106.238.22) — 9 agents, 20 cron jobs, Risktaker (equities) + Bolz (options) are primary
type: project
---

Marcel (18, Zurich, apprentice at Kyndryl) runs a multi-agent trading system on his monolith server via Tailscale (ssh monolith).

**Setup:** OpenClaw v2026.2.23 at `~/.openclaw/` on monolith
**Config:** `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` + `~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json`

**Key ag

- [project_team.md](project_team.md) — Team roles: Julian (lead, non-engineer), Marcel + Nepomuk (engineers), Lena (end user)
- [user_julian.md](user_julian.md) — Julian from Butel KlG, development lead, prefers discussion before implementation
- [project_arbor_openclaw.md](project_arbor_openclaw.md) — Multi-agent AI system for Arbor Capital PE fund, deployed to EC2 "arbor", repo at Butel-KlG/arbor-openclaw

name: Arbor Capital OpenClaw Agent System
description: Multi-agent AI system for Arbor Capital PE fund on OpenClaw, deployed to EC2 "arbor" server, repo at Butel-KlG/arbor-openclaw
type: project
---

- **Repo**: github.com/Butel-KlG/arbor-openclaw (private), cloned locally at `/home/sysadmin/Projects/arbor-openclaw`
- **Server**: EC2 `arbor` (13.48.26.56), Ubuntu 24.04, 2 vCPU / 8GB RAM
- **Platform**: OpenClaw v2026.3.9 at `/opt/openclaw/` (pnpm-managed, don't touch)
- **Custom configs**: `/opt/arbor/config/` on server, version-controlled in git
- **Runtime config**: `/home/ubuntu/.openclaw/openclaw.json` (deployed from repo, contains secrets — NOT in git)
- **Python env**: `/home/ubuntu/.o

- **5 agents**: 1 orchestrator + 4 specialists
- **ClawdBot** (agent "main") — orchestrator, routes via Telegram, manages voting gates + quality verification
- **Researcher** (agent "research") — deep web research, sourced data only, reusable across flows
- **SectorScorer** (agent "scorer") — 22-metric scoring framework + qualitative PE assessment
- **IMReviewer** (agent "im") — PDF parsing (pdfplumber) + financial extraction + deal assessment
- **ExcelWriter** (agent "excel") — openpyxl output,

- `sector_scorer_claude_skill.md` (51KB) at `/mnt/c/Users/Admin/Downloads/arbor/` — source for skill splits
- `20260306_Rollup_Framework_v2.xlsx` — master scoring workbook (Template + Dashboard + per-sector sheets)
- `Project SPIRIT - Information Memorandum.pdf` (3.5MB) — test IM document
- OneDrive credentials at `/tmp/arbor_zip_explore/two/OneDrive/OneDrive/credentials.txt` — Phase 2

- **Lena** (Arbor Capital) — primary user, sent process map + requirements email
- **Julian** (Butel KlG) — development lead
- **Nepomuk** (nepomuk5665) — co-developer

- **Phase 1** (current): 5-agent architecture, anti-hallucination, skill split, test with local files
- **Phase 2**: OneDrive integration (Graph API, credentials available)
- **Phase 3**: Recursive learning refinement, RAG pipeline

name: Team roles
description: Who does what on the Arbor Capital / OpenClaw project — engineering vs. business roles
type: project
---

- **Julian** (Arbor Capital) — stakeholder, not an engineer, not from Butel KlG
- **Lena** (Arbor Capital) — primary end user (PE professional, not technical)
- **Marcel** (Butel KlG) — engineer
- **Nepomuk** (`nepomuk5665`, Butel KlG) — engineer

**Why:** System changes and behavioral modifications to ClawdBot should be routed to Marcel/Nepomuk (engineers at Bu

name: Julian - Arbor Capital stakeholder
description: Julian is a stakeholder at Arbor Capital, not an engineer — prefers discussion before implementation
type: user
---

- Stakeholder at Arbor Capital Partners LLP (the client)
- NOT part of Butel KlG (that's Marcel and Nepomuk)
- Prefers to discuss architecture decisions before implementation rather than jumping straight in
- Interested in ruflo-style recursive learning patterns for agent improvement
- Values having a proper repo structure and

- `COMMISSION_RATE` canonical source: `services/backend/src/common/constants/payment.constants.ts`
- All constants barrel-exported from `services/backend/src/common/constants/index.ts`
- Import as: `import { COMMISSION_RATE } from '../common/constants';`

- Analytics uses Europe/Zurich timezone via `date-fns-tz` (Sprint 2)
- `analytics-date.util.ts` uses `toZonedTime`/`fromZonedTime` for Swiss midnight alignment
- `analytics.service.ts` `formatDateKey` uses `format()` with `{ timeZone: 'Europe/Zurich' }`
- `date-fns-tz` exports available: `toZonedTime`, `fromZonedTime`, `format`

- Full suite: 63 suites, ~1340 tests, ~46s
- Backend tests: `npm test` from `services/backend/`
- Targeted: `npx jest --no-coverage <pattern>`
- ERROR logs in test output are expected (from mocked error scenarios in spec files)

- `OrderStatus` Prisma enum replaces old String field (migration: `20260205_order_status_enum`)
- Enum values: `AWAITING_PAYMENT`, `AUTHORIZED`, `TIME_CONFLICT`, `CONFIRMED`, `RECEIVED`, `COMPLETED`, `PAYMENT_FAILED`, `PAYMENT_TOO_LATE`, `EXPIRED`, `CANCELLED`, `REJECTED`
- Status groups in `common/constants/order-status.ts`: `ACTIVE_STATUSES`, `TERMINAL_STATUSES`, `KITCHEN_VISIBLE_STATUSES`, `MODIFIABLE_STATUSES`
- Import: `import { OrderStatus } from '@prisma/client'` or `from '../common/const

- `DATABASE_URL` is NOT in `.env` — it's built dynamically by `scripts/utils/env.js` during `pnpm dev`
- For standalone Prisma commands: `DATABASE_URL="postgresql://<POSTGRES_USER>:<DB_PASSWORD_RAW>@localhost:5432/<POSTGRES_DB>" pnpm exec prisma ...`
- Get credentials from root `.env`: `POSTGRES_USER`, `DB_PASSWORD_RAW`, `POSTGRES_DB`
- Migration drift on `20260216_schema_sync` — `prisma migrate dev` wants to reset DB; use `--create-only` or create migration manually

- Sprint 1: AbandonmentMetrics, Refund cascade, cleanup statuses, audit retention, peakAbandonmentHour dead code
- Sprint 2: COMMISSION_RATE centralization, analytics timezone (Europe/Zurich)
- Sprint 3: Order Status Enum redesign (String→Prisma enum, all services + frontends migrated)
- Sprint 4 planned: Brain-Analytics overlap refactor, Brain module timezone

name: openpyxl chart gotchas
description: Hard-won lessons from debugging openpyxl chart generation for the Rollup Framework Excel
type: feedback
---

openpyxl 3.1.5 chart generation has several non-obvious pitfalls:

1. **BubbleChart corrupts drawing XML on existing worksheets** — works on fresh workbooks but produces "Removed Part: drawing1.xml" error when added to sheets that previously had charts. Use ScatterChart instead.

2. **Scatter series `dLbls.showLegendKey` must be explicitly False**
File unknown was edited 48 times this session — likely a hot path worth monitoring.
